skill.name: firebase-ai-logic-basics
skill.description: Guides developers through Firebase AI Logic setup and Gemini API usage for web, Android, iOS, and Flutter apps.
skill.summary: Add Gemini-powered AI features to Firebase apps using platform setup guides and SDK usage patterns.
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: AI review found the static findings are false positives caused by Markdown documentation, fenced code examples, and Firebase documentation links. The CLI commands are setup instructions for Firebase projects, the network references point to official Firebase documentation, and the weak cryptography matches are keyword collisions on Gemini API text. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, credential harvesting, or malicious execution path was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Setup Commands
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static analysis flagged inline Markdown commands such as npm, npx, flutterfire, and SDK setup snippets. These are user-facing Firebase setup instructions, not hidden code that the skill executes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched text appears inside Markdown documentation and fenced setup examples. No file contains Ruby code, shell wrappers, or an automatic execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Firebase Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static analysis flagged hardcoded URLs, but they are links to Firebase documentation and release notes. They do not transmit local data or secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs point to firebase.google.com documentation and are presented as references. No network client, webhook, or exfiltration behavior is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Match
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static analysis flagged weak cryptography on lines that discuss Gemini API usage and a sample stock symbol. No MD5, SHA-1, encryption routine, or password hashing code was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found API documentation text and a function parameter example, not cryptographic implementation. The scanner likely matched short tokens in prose or sample identifiers.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Prompt Injection Not Found
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Review looked for override language, fake system messages, pre-approval claims, and requests to skip analysis. No evidence found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted search and contextual review did not find instruction override text. The files contain product guidance, warnings, and SDK examples within the stated skill scope.

content.value_statement: Adding Gemini features to Firebase apps can be confusing across web and mobile platforms. This skill gives setup steps, SDK patterns, and safety guidance for Firebase AI Logic.
actual_capabilities: Guides Firebase AI Logic setup for web, Android, iOS, and Flutter projects.
Shows text generation, chat sessions, streaming responses, and multimodal input patterns.
Explains when to use the Gemini Developer API or Vertex AI Gemini API.
Documents App Check, Remote Config, and backend provisioning requirements.
Provides examples for structured output, function calling, and hybrid on-device inference.
limitations: Does not implement production backend services or Cloud Functions.
Does not replace official Firebase billing, quota, or release documentation.
Code samples are reference patterns and may need updates for project versions.
Does not provide model fine-tuning or advanced prompt evaluation workflows.

use_cases.title: Add AI to a web app
use_cases.description: Use Firebase AI Logic setup steps to initialize the web SDK and call Gemini models from a client app.

use_cases.title: Prototype mobile AI features
use_cases.description: Follow Android, iOS, or Flutter examples for text generation, chat, streaming, and multimodal input.

use_cases.title: Review Firebase AI architecture
use_cases.description: Compare provider choices, App Check requirements, and Remote Config guidance before planning production rollout.

prompt_templates.title: Start a web setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up Firebase AI Logic in a web app. Include the required Firebase initialization steps and first Gemini call.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new web project

prompt_templates.title: Add mobile generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to add Firebase AI Logic text generation to my Android, iOS, or Flutter app. Ask which platform I use first.

prompt_templates.title: Design multimodal input
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me add image or file input to a Firebase AI Logic feature. Explain client limits and when to use Cloud Storage URLs.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare production safeguards
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Firebase AI Logic plan for production. Focus on App Check, model selection, Remote Config, and provider choice.

output_examples.input: I need to enable Firebase AI Logic before coding my Flutter app.
output_examples.output: The response explains that backend provisioning comes first, then FlutterFire client configuration, Firebase initialization, authentication, and model creation.

output_examples.input: I want a web chat feature that remembers earlier messages.
output_examples.output: The response describes creating a chat session, seeding history with user and model turns, sending new messages, and displaying responses.

output_examples.input: I need safer production settings for Gemini in Firebase.
output_examples.output: The response recommends App Check, Remote Config for model names, clear provider selection, and safety settings where the SDK supports them.

best_practices: Run Firebase AI Logic backend provisioning before relying on client SDK configuration.
Use App Check to protect API quota and reduce unauthorized client usage.
Use Remote Config for model names so updates do not require app redeploys.
anti_patterns: Skipping backend provisioning and expecting client configuration to enable the service.
Hardcoding model names throughout application code without an update strategy.
Choosing Vertex AI Gemini API for prototypes that only need the Gemini Developer API.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps you add Gemini-powered features to Firebase web and mobile apps using Firebase AI Logic.

faq.question: Which platforms are covered?
faq.answer: The skill covers web, Android, iOS, and Flutter. It also mentions Unity support at a high level.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents commands that a developer may run during Firebase project setup.

faq.question: Does it include security guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It highlights App Check, backend provisioning, provider selection, and Remote Config for model names.

faq.question: Can it help with multimodal inputs?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains patterns for images, media files, PDFs, inline data, and Cloud Storage URLs for larger files.

faq.question: Is this a fine-tuning skill?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on Firebase AI Logic SDK integration and common Gemini usage patterns.
